title: Change List Items
content:
### Changing Items

Lists are **mutable** -- you can change individual items by index.

---

### Change a Single Item

```python
fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
fruits[1] = "blackberry"
print(fruits)  # ["apple", "blackberry", "cherry"]
```

---

### Change a Range of Items

```python
fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry", "orange", "kiwi"]

# Replace items at index 1 and 2
fruits[1:3] = ["blackberry", "watermelon"]
print(fruits)  # ["apple", "blackberry", "watermelon", "orange", "kiwi"]

# Replace with more items than the range
fruits[1:2] = ["blueberry", "raspberry"]
print(fruits)  # ["apple", "blueberry", "raspberry", "watermelon", "orange", "kiwi"]

# Replace with fewer items
fruits[1:4] = ["grape"]
print(fruits)  # ["apple", "grape", "orange", "kiwi"]
```

---

### Insert Without Replacing

Use `.insert()` to add at a specific position:

```python
fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
fruits.insert(1, "blueberry")
print(fruits)  # ["apple", "blueberry", "banana", "cherry"]
```

---

> **Key Point:** When you assign a slice, the replacement list can be a different size. Items shift to accommodate.
codeExample:
fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
fruits[1] = "blackberry"
print("Changed:", fruits)

fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry", "orange", "kiwi"]
fruits[1:3] = ["grape", "melon"]
print("Range changed:", fruits)

fruits.insert(0, "mango")
print("Inserted:", fruits)

quiz.question: How do you change the second item in a list?
